_comment: Parallel build lanes as VS Code INTEGRATED-TERMINAL tabs (the loop runs in VS Code, never a separate macOS Terminal window). Cmd+Shift+P -> 'Tasks: Run Task' -> pick a lane (or 'Lanes 1-4: open all'). Each opens a dedicated integrated terminal in its worktree and starts the scoped autonomous /loop. The args string is a natural-language bootstrap because a slash command cannot be passed at launch. Lanes 1-2 still point at cadence-cockpit / cadence-knowledge until those running worktrees are migrated to cadence-lane-1 / cadence-lane-2 (see PARALLEL-BUILD.md 'Migrating the folder names'); update the cwd here when you migrate them.
_bootstrap: Shared kickoff: run as a continuous loop in THIS terminal, claim atomically before building, never stop after one item.

tasks.label: Lane 1
args: You are scoped parallel build Lane 1 in this worktree. Read .remember/LANE.md and docs/operations/autonomous-build-loop.md sections 15-16, then run as a CONTINUOUS autonomous loop (equivalent to /loop) IN THIS TERMINAL. Each cycle: git fetch + rebase origin/main; bash scripts/lane.sh reap; SELECT the next highest-impact eligible item live from feature-dashboard.md (prefer this lane's categories then roam; skip blocked/deferred/founder-gated/already-claimed; cross-check bash scripts/lane.sh list); CLAIM IT ATOMICALLY with bash scripts/lane.sh claim <ID> 1 "<globs>" before writing any code (if it prints HELD or CONFLICT, pick another); flip the dashboard row to In Dev and push that claim first; build; gate (bunx tsc --noEmit + bun run build + tests); adversarially review; run the doc-loop; commit explicit paths with a WHY; fast-forward push; flip the row done and bash scripts/lane.sh release <ID>; then immediately continue to the next item. Never stop after one build. If the whole board is dry, write 'board dry - long-polling' to the lane report and recheck in ~25 min. Stay in lane via the ledger; never touch FORBIDDEN files.
